name: Rosemary-Olive Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 4 Oil-packed dried tomatoes, drained and cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 450°F. Place dough in a bowl. Add 3 tbsp olive oil and minced fresh rosemary (or 1 tsp dried rosemary). Season generously with pepper.
Knead the dough until all ingredients are well combined. Roll the dough out on a lightly floured work surface into a 9x6-inch rectangle.
Transfer the rolled-out dough to a baking sheet. Flatten and press it into a 12x9-inch rectangle. Rub 3 tbsp olive oil over the top.
Sprinkle 3/4 cup Parmesan over; gently press it into the dough. Bake until the bread is almost cooked through and the cheese begins to brown, about 12 minutes.
Arrange olives, tomatoes, and garlic on top of the bread. Sprinkle the remaining Parmesan to lightly cover the surface. Drizzle with the remaining 3 tbsp olive oil.
Top with fresh rosemary sprigs (if using). Continue baking until the cheese melts and the bread is fully cooked through, about 5 minutes. Cut into squares or wedges and serve.
